display.purpose: Brand wordmark (Bizify logo) in navbar and footer only. Never used for UI headings.

headline.purpose: Section heroes and page titles. One per screen — the single loudest voice.

title.purpose: Card headings, modal titles, section intros. Subordinate to Headline.

body.purpose: All prose and descriptions. Capped at 65–75ch. Light subheadings (font-light, 1.125rem) pair with normal-weight body.

label.purpose: Button text, field labels, nav links, badge text, tab labels.

shadows.name: ambient-card
shadows.purpose: Always-on base depth for cards and containers. Establishes visual hierarchy at rest.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover state for interactive cards, paired with translateY(-6px).

shadows.name: cta-amber-glow
shadows.purpose: Default state for primary gradient (amber) button.

shadows.name: cta-amber-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover state for primary gradient button.

shadows.name: cta-cyan-glow
shadows.purpose: Default state for secondary gradient (cyan) button.

shadows.name: cta-cyan-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover state for secondary gradient button.

shadows.name: subtle-ring
shadows.purpose: Card ring (ring-1 ring-foreground/10) for containers where full shadow would be heavy.

motion.name: ease-out-smooth
motion.purpose: Default easing for scroll animations and entrance transitions (--anim-easing).

motion.name: duration-hover
motion.purpose: Hover state transitions for buttons and cards (--hover-duration).

motion.name: stagger-delay
motion.purpose: Per-child delay for staggered entrance sequences (--stagger-delay).

components.name: Primary Button (Amber Gradient)
components.description: The system's one forward-motion signal. Used on the single most important CTA per screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; background: linear-gradient(to right, #F59E0B, #EAB308); color: #1C1C1E; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; padding: 8px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(245,158,11,0.4); transition: transform 250ms ease, box-shadow 250ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(245,158,11,0.6); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(245,158,11,0.3); transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(245,158,11,0.4); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button (Cyan Gradient)
components.description: Second-tier CTA. Orientation and progression, subordinate to amber.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; background: linear-gradient(to bottom right, #0891B2, #06B6D4); color: #FFFFFF; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; padding: 8px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(87,222,255,0.4); transition: transform 250ms ease, box-shadow 250ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(87,222,255,0.6); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:active { box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(87,222,255,0.3); transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(6,182,212,0.4); } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Quiet tertiary action. Used for nav links, icon buttons, and actions that should not compete.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; background: transparent; color: #1C1C1E; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; padding: 8px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #F5F5F5; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(6,182,212,0.3); } .ds-btn-ghost:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Standard content card for dashboard features, ideas, and service tiles.
components.html:
Market Analysis

Understand your target market and competitive landscape before you build.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; padding: 16px; background: #FAFAFA; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px -2px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); transition: transform 250ms ease, box-shadow 250ms ease, border-color 250ms ease; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-6px); box-shadow: 0 20px 30px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); } .ds-card-icon { width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 8px; background: #0891B2; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-card-title { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1C1C1E; margin: 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #8C8C8C; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form text field. Clarity Cyan owns the focus state.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-label { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #1C1C1E; } .ds-input { height: 36px; width: 100%; padding: 0 12px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E9E9E9; border-radius: 10px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #1C1C1E; outline: none; transition: border-color 200ms ease, box-shadow 200ms ease; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8C8C8C; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #0891B2; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(6,182,212,0.2); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Fixed top navigation. Brand name in Cormorant SC; CTAs right-aligned.
components.html:
Bizify
FeaturesPricing
components.css: .ds-nav { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 50; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 12px 24px; background: #FAFAFA; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(28,28,30,0.08); } .ds-nav-brand { font-family: 'Cormorant SC', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 400; color: #1C1C1E; letter-spacing: 0.02em; } .ds-nav-links { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; } .ds-nav-link { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #595959; text-decoration: none; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 8px; transition: color 200ms ease, background 200ms ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #1C1C1E; background: #F5F5F5; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pill badge for plan tiers, status labels, and active filter chips. Amber variant marks featured/active states.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 20px; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; background: #F5F5F5; color: #595959; } .ds-badge-amber { background: #FEF3C7; color: #92400E; } .ds-badge-cyan { background: #ECFEFF; color: #164E63; }

components.name: Process Step Card
components.description: Signature onboarding component. Guided step with amber step badge. Used in onboarding flows and process sections.
components.html:
Step 1

Validate Your Idea

Answer five quick questions to see if your concept has legs before you invest more time.
components.css: .ds-process-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; padding: 16px 20px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.6); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.7); border-radius: 14px; transition: background 200ms ease; } .ds-process-card:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); } .ds-step-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; width: fit-content; height: 22px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(245,158,11,0.5); background: #FFFBEB; color: #92400E; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-process-title { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1C1C1E; margin: 0; } .ds-process-body { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #8C8C8C; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview:
Bizify is built for the moment before confidence exists. The Cairo student opening the dashboard isn't looking for features — she's looking for permission to begin. The design system's single job is to hand her the map. Every screen has a primary action that reads before she consciously chooses it. Every surface is warm enough to linger and clear enough to act.

The color strategy is Restrained: tinted achromatic neutrals carry 90% of every surface. Confidence Amber appears only on elements that mean "go here next." Clarity Cyan handles feedback and orientation. Neither color competes. Their reserve is what makes them readable.

This system explicitly rejects the Salesforce/SAP enterprise density, the Notion/Linear cold white minimalism, and the hustler SaaS dark dashboard with neon gradients.
keyCharacteristics: Warm achromatic neutrals: Paper (#FAFAFA) not pure white, Ink (#1C1C1E) not pure black
One forward-motion signal (Confidence Amber), one orientation signal (Clarity Cyan)
Rounded, comfortable components with generous touch targets (44px minimum)
Ambient shadows establish visual hierarchy at rest before any interaction
One primary action per screen; progressive disclosure over feature completeness
RTL/LTR layout mirroring designed from day zero, not retrofitted

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Confidence Amber appears on at most one primary action per screen. If amber is visible in two places simultaneously, one of them is competing for attention that should be undivided.

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: Paper (#FAFAFA) and Ink (#1C1C1E) are the canonical background and text. Pure #FFFFFF and #000000 are prohibited. The tint matters.

rules.name: The One Display Rule
rules.body: Cormorant SC is reserved for the brand wordmark. Mixing it into UI headings or marketing copy dilutes the brand signal.

rules.name: The Hierarchy-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Cards and containers carry a permanent ambient shadow. The user should read the visual hierarchy before any interaction. Shadows that appear only on hover create interfaces that require exploration to understand.

dos: Do use Confidence Amber on exactly one primary CTA per screen.
Do use Clarity Cyan for all focus rings, input confirmation borders, and selected states.
Do use Paper (#FAFAFA) as the base background. Never pure #FFFFFF.
Do maintain 44px touch targets on interactive elements in mobile-first layouts.
Do cap body line length at 65–75ch.
Do apply ambient shadows to cards and containers at rest.
Do put one clear primary action per screen. Surface the next step; hide the rest.
Do design RTL layout mirroring before it breaks, not after.
donts: Don't build dense dashboards with every feature visible at once. If it looks like Salesforce or SAP, it wasn't built for this user.
Don't use pure #FFFFFF backgrounds or #000000 text. Paper and Ink only.
Don't import cold minimalism: white surfaces, micro-typography, zero warmth. If it feels like Notion or Linear, it doesn't feel like Bizify.
Don't use dark mode with neon amber/cyan gradients or the hustler SaaS aesthetic.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or alerts.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient background).
Don't place two gradient buttons side by side. One amber gradient action per screen.
Don't use glassmorphism as a default style. Reserved for overlay panels and the process-step card only.
Don't reach for modals as a first response. Exhaust inline and progressive disclosure alternatives first.
